title: Robots Exclusion Protocol, Failure Semantics, Caching, and Sitemaps · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved crawl trace contains one plausible authorization, URL, robots, HTTP, decoding, parser, selector, frontier, concurrency, metadata, accessibility, privacy, export, browser, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in RFC 9309 robots and sitemap decision record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Fetch `/robots.txt` at the correct service origin and use an honest stable product token and contact identity.
Apply RFC 9309 group selection, rule matching, percent encoding, longest-match precedence, and allow-over-disallow tie behavior.
Handle successful, unavailable, unreachable, redirect, oversized, and stale robots responses with explicit cache and conservative stop policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Sitemap Boundaries
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-sitemap-boundaries-pojzvj" that uses case 2084 to validate RFC 9309 matching and failure behavior plus bounded same-authority sitemap discovery. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Sitemap Boundaries.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Sitemap Boundaries into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Sitemap Boundaries, keep Sitemap Boundaries connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-sitemap-boundaries-pojzvj
# Competency: scraper-sitemap-boundaries.
# Case 2084: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_sitemap_boundaries_pojzvj(robot_cases: int = 16, correct_decisions: int = 16, failure_modes: int = 5, sitemap_urls: int = 40, cross_host_rejections: int = 3) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_pojzvj = "pojzvj-jvzjop-2084"
    assert evidence_variant_pojzvj
    evidence_axes_pojzvj = (
        "authorizationpojzvj",
        "url2084",
        "responsejvzjop",
        "treepojzvj2084",
        "frontier2084pojzvj",
        "reportjvzjop2084",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_pojzvj)) == 6
    if robot_cases < 8 or correct_decisions < 0:
        return False, correct_decisions, "robots-cases"
    if correct_decisions != robot_cases:
        return False, correct_decisions, "robots-decision"
    if failure_modes < 4:
        return False, failure_modes, "robots-failure-policy"
    if sitemap_urls < 1 or cross_host_rejections < 1:
        return False, cross_host_rejections, "sitemap-boundary"
    assert correct_decisions == robot_cases
    return True, correct_decisions, "bounded-robots-sitemap-policy"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Sitemap Boundaries structural check is only one gate. RFC 9309 robots and sitemap decision record must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
